commands.review_summary:
The PR relaxes the embedded agent session-fence rewrite guard so equal-line-count rewrites reach the existing linear transcript comparison instead of immediately failing as takeovers.

PR surface: Source 0. Total 0 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main rejects a same-inode, same-size, equal-line rewrite after fingerprint metadata changes because the append path requires growth and the rewrite path exits on `<=`. I did not establish a fresh live Telegram or Matrix reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs contributor or maintainer proof plus focused session-fence coverage before merge; automation should not take over while external real behavior proof is missing.

target.title: fix(embedded-agent-runner): treat the run's own no-op in-place session rewrite as benign
target.body:
## Problem

On long, streaming agentic turns (notably Matrix/Telegram channels with `streaming:partial`), the embedded agent intermittently aborts with `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError: session file changed while embedded prompt lock was released`, and the already-streamed reply is redacted ("Message deleted"). Related: #91699.

## Root cause (confirmed by instrumentation)

During the `releaseForPrompt()` window the session-persistence layer rewrites the whole `.jsonl` **in place with identical content** ã same inode, same size, **same line count** ã only `mtime`/`ctime` change. A diagnostic dumped right before the throw on a real takeover:

```
sameIdentity:true  snapSize==currSize==507106  endsNL:true  appendHead:null  ownedGen:null
```

At `reacquireAfterPrompt ã assertSessionFileFence`, every exemption then fails:

- `sameSessionFileFingerprint` sees the `mtime`/`ctime` delta ã not "unchanged".
- the owned-write exemption is empty (no owned write published this window).
- `sessionFenceAdvanceIsBenign` requires the file to have **grown** ã `readAppendedSessionFileText` returns `undefined` when `current.size <= previous.size` ã `false`.
- `sessionFenceRewriteIsBenign` bails at `if (currentLines.length <= previousLines.length) return false;` because the no-op rewrite has the **same** line count.

ã false `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError`. It is intermittent because it only fires when such a same-content rewrite is the last modification before reacquire.

## Fix

Change the rewrite line-count guard from `<=` to `<` so an equal-line-count rewrite proceeds to the existing linear-migration check:

- a content-preserving rewrite ã all previous lines migrate, zero new lines (`currentLines.slice(previousLines.length)` is empty) ã benign;
- a genuine external takeover (different lines, even at the same count) ã `lineMatchesLinearTranscriptMigration` fails ã still detected;
- a shrinking rewrite (`<`) is still rejected.

**No loss of detection.**

## Verification

Reproduced reliably with long multi-tool turns (~1 takeover per 3 turns). After this one-character change: **0 takeovers across 15 long turns / ~25 min**.

Orthogonal to #91699's owned-write publication: even with `message_end`/compaction publishing owned, the no-op rewrite during the window still trips this guard.
target.author: gucasbrg
